article_title: CPython Targets Python 3.16 for First Rust Code as Project Clears Build System Hurdle and Plots PEP Timeline

content_preview.title: CPython Targets Python 3.16 for First Rust Code as Project Clears Build System Hurdle and Plots PEP Timeline
content_preview.summary: The Rust for CPython project has achieved cross-platform builds in CI and shifted its target from Python 3.15 to 3.16, setting a July deadline to submit a formal Python Enhancement Proposal that would make Rust a build-time dependency of the reference interpreter.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The effort to introduce Rust into CPython, Python's reference interpreter, has reached its first concrete engineering milestone. The Rust for CPython project announced in an [April 2026 progress update](https://blog.python.org/2026/04/rust-for-cpython-2026-04/) that its fork of CPython now builds successfully with Rust across all tested platforms in continuous integration, clearing a prerequisite that had blocked forward progress for months.

The project has also revised its timelin...
